title: Differential scanning calorimetry and infrared imaging
abstract: A method of characterising a sample, the method comprising the steps of: performing differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) on the sample; and, while the sample is undergoing DSC, taking infrared images of the sample.
claims: Claims CLAIMS 1. A method of characterising a sample, the method comprising the steps of: - performing differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) on the sample; and - while the sample is undergoing DSC, taking infrared images of the sample. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the method is performed using an apparatus for performing differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) on the sample, and wherein: - the apparatus comprises a housing configured to contain the sample during DSC; and - the apparatus comprises an infrared imaging device adapted to receive infrared radiation emitted by the sample during DSC measurement and/or the housing comprises a window pane made of a material that is transparent to infrared radiation. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the method further comprises using the images and data obtained by the DSC to calculate the entropy of each thermal image, or a section thereof, using the following formula: wherein: H is the entropy; and ^^ ^^ is the probability of a specific heat flow value from the DSC. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the entropy is determined according to the formula: where: H is the entropy; p(x i ) is the probability of occurrence of the outcome x i ; and n is the total number of distinct outcomes. 5. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the method further comprises calculating the thermal gradient using the following formula: wherein: | â ^^| represents the temperature at a given point in the dataset; and are the partial derivatives of the temperature with respect to the x and y coordinates, respectively. 6. The method of claim 3 or any one of claims 4 and 5 as dependent upon claim 3, wherein the method further comprises determining the free energy change for mixing for the sample, for example using Flory-Huggins theory. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the free energy change for mixing for the sample is determined using the following formula: ^^ â² = ^^ + ^^ ^^ wherein: xâ is the Flory-Huggins interaction parameter; x is the Flory-Huggins interaction parameter; Î± is the proportionality constant; and H is the entropy. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the Flory-Huggins interaction parameter ^^ is determined from the slope of a plot of : against Î¦2 polymer, wherein: Tfmix is the melting point onset of the drug in the polymer drug mixture, T fpure is the melting point onset of the pure drug, â Hf is the enthalpy of fusion of the pure drug, R is the gas constant, ^^ ^^ ^^ ^^ ^^ is the volume fraction of the drug, ^^ ^^ ^^ ^^ ^^ ^^ ^^ ^^ is the volume fraction of the polymer, and r is the ratio of the molecular volume of the polymer to the molecular v olume of the drug. 9. The method of claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the modified Flory-Huggins free energy of mixing is calculated using the following formula: âG m â² = kT(n 1 lnÏ 1 + n 2 lnÏ 2 + n 1 Ï 2 xâ²) wherein: âG m â² is the modified Flory-Huggins free energy of mixing, k is Boltzmannâs constant, T is the bulk temperature, ^^ ^^ and ^^ ^^ are the volume fractions of the drug and polymer, respectively, ^^ ^^ and ^^ ^^ are the number of moles of the drug and polymer, respectively, and ^^â² is the modified Flory-Huggins interaction parameter. 10. The method of claim 3 or any one of claims 4 to 9 as dependent upon claim 3, wherein the thermal image, or the section thereof, comprises a plurality of temperature points, and wherein the method comprises rounding each temperature point to the nearest value at a given spacing of from 0.001Â°C to 2.0Â°C. 11. The method of claim 3 or any one of claims 4 to 10 as dependent upon claim 3, wherein the thermal image, or the section thereof, comprises a plurality of temperature points, and wherein the method comprises calculating the Shannon entropy for a plurality of windows of two or more adjacent temperature points from a thermal image or the section thereof, and calculating the mean average of the Shannon entropy of the windows of the thermal image or the section thereof. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein each window comprises four or more adjacent temperature points. 13. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the sample comprises a biologically active agent and one or more polymers. 14. A method of processing data, the method comprising calculating, for a sample, entropy and/or the thermal gradient, wherein the calculation uses data obtained from one or more infrared images or a section of one or more infrared images of the sample. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the method comprises: - determining the Shannon entropy from the one or more infrared images or the section thereof; - determining the traditional Flory-Huggins interaction parameter from the data obtained by DSC; and - combining the Shannon entropy with the traditional Flory-Huggins interaction parameter to determine a modified Flory-Huggins interaction parameter. 16. An apparatus for performing differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) on a sample, wherein the apparatus comprises a housing configured to contain the sample during DSC, and wherein the apparatus comprises an infrared imaging device adapted to receive infrared radiation emitted by the sample during DSC measurement and/or the housing comprises a window pane made of a material that is transparent to infrared radiation. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the infrared imaging device has a sensor that comprises 100 pixels or more. 18. The apparatus of claim 16 or claim 17, wherein the infrared imaging device is a thermal camera. 19. A cover for differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) of a sample, wherein the cover is made of a material that is transparent to infrared radiation. 20. The cover of claim 19, wherein the cover: - is substantially cylindrical, - has a diameter of from 2mm to 30mm, and - has a thickness of from 0.05mm to 5mm. 21. A kit for containing a sample during differential scanning calorimetry (DSC), the kit comprising: - a cover according to claim 19 or claim 20, and - a pan. 22. The kit of claim 21, wherein the pan is made of aluminium, alumina, copper, gold, graphite, quartz, stainless steel, titanium, and/or platinum. 23. The cover or the kit of any one of claims 19 to 22, wherein the cover consists of a material that is transparent to infrared radiation. 24. The apparatus, the cover or the kit of any one of claims 16 to 23, wherein the material that is transparent to infrared radiation is BaF 2 , CdTe, GaAs, Ge, thallium bromoiodide, ZnSe, ZnS, infrared plastic, and/or a glass of germanium, arsenic and selenium. 25. The apparatus, the cover or the kit of claim 24, wherein the material is zinc selenide.
